intro: Rectangle rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Slint. Slint is positioned for embedded and desktop teams wanting a modern rust-first declarative gui toolkit, while Rectangle leans toward macos users who want keyboard-driven window snapping without config hassle. In practice, Declarative GUI toolkit for Rust, C++, JavaScript, and Python targeting embedded, desktop, and web. Free open-source window manager for macOS with keyboard shortcuts and drag-to-snap — based on Spectacle. Slint runs on freemium with a free tier; Rectangle runs on free with a free tier. Rectangle rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Slint. Listed strengths unique to Slint include declarative gui that compiles to native, while Rectangle lists free open-source window snapping for macos on its side. Reach for Slint when you need rust desktop gui app with declarative slint ui language; reach for Rectangle when the job is macos window snapping with keyboard shortcuts and drag zones. The honest trade-off: Slint is not built for projects needing mature widgets and battle-tested mobile cross-platform support, and Rectangle is not the right call for power users who want full i3-style.
quick_take: Slint is for embedded and desktop teams wanting a modern rust-first declarative gui toolkit; Rectangle is for macos users who want keyboard-driven window snapping without config hassle; decide based on.
verdict_a: Choose Slint if embedded and desktop teams wanting a modern rust-first declarative gui toolkit and you need a free tier to validate fit.
verdict_b: Choose Rectangle if macos users who want keyboard-driven window snapping without config hassle and you need a free tier to validate fit.

faq.a: Both Slint and Rectangle expose free tiers with no headline entry price listed. The real comparison happens once you cross the free limits, and that pricing isn't captured in the summary data. Check each tool's pricing page for your volume band.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Slint to Rectangle?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Slint is relatively light thanks to its low lock-in, and onboarding to Rectangle should be straightforward given its low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Slint or Rectangle?
faq.a: Both Slint and Rectangle score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis alone. The real differentiator usually lands in SDK fit for your language, error handling, and docs depth. A half-day integration spike tells you more than the score.

faq.q: Is Rectangle a good alternative to Slint?
faq.a: Rectangle is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is macos users who want keyboard-driven window snapping without config hassle, while Slint's is embedded and desktop teams wanting a modern rust-first declarative gui toolkit. If your job matches Slint's profile more closely, switching to Rectangle is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: Rectangle is explicitly not for power users who want full i3-style tiling window management.
